what_it_does: Browser-console prove-on-one harness that resolves a decision address, checks whether the decision card is mounted (real or synthetic), asserts an in-card ask is present, and optionally exercises the real take path to verify the decision state flips and the host re-renders the card.
observations: Async function that returns { pass, mode, legs:[{leg, ok, detail}] }
Auto-detects 'real-card' mode by checking #gallery-mount for a non-stub child; otherwise falls back to 'synthetic' by injecting a stub and dispatching decision:rendered
Calls /api/territory?address=<addr> to fetch the decision record and again after a take to verify state
Uses CustomEvents decision:rendered and gallery:verb, and optionally window.galleryBinder.decide
Has fixed wait delays of 700ms and 1400ms
Guards the take leg: opts.decide is required and comments warn to use it only on throwaway decisions
Exports to window.proveDecision and module.exports for CommonJS
standouts: Per-leg verdict structure with null for skipped legs
Real-card vs synthetic auto-detection so the harness can run before or after a fork render
Explicit safety design: default run only verifies resolve/host/ask and leaves the real choice to the operator
conventions: Header comment with timestamp and usage examples
Async/await with try/catch on the resolve fetch
Feature detection for galleryBinder and DOM elements
Global window assignment plus CommonJS module.exports fallback
concerns: Relies on brittle CSS selectors and hard-coded timeouts
Synthetic mode clears #gallery-mount innerHTML, which would destroy any real card already present
In-card ask detection falls back to innerText.includes('Ask'), which can false-positive
No cleanup of the stub element or event listeners after the run
Re-fetch after take is not wrapped in try/catch
notes: A lead-paste console harness for same-second, per-leg validation of a decision-card fork, with a deliberate safe default that avoids flipping real state.
questions: What is the exact contract of window.galleryBinder.decide?
Which host events are expected to fire after a take, and does gallery:verb reliably trigger them?
How are option labels matched against idy.options?
What injects the .brain-ask element and when?
What is the full /api/territory response schema beyond identity?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is described as a READY prove-on-one harness and it implements exactly that flow with resolve, host, ask, take, and refresh legs.
intention_signals: Provide a single, self-contained console command a lead can paste to validate a decision card end-to-end in the live surface, while keeping real decisions under human control and only exercising the take path on disposable decisions.
novelty: Combines per-leg pass/fail/null verdicts, real-vs-synthetic card detection, and an explicit safety guard that separates read-only verification from the state-mutating take path.
contribution: A reusable browser-side prove-on-one harness that records leg-level results and supports both safe verification and optional throwaway take validation.
summary_for_embedding: A browser console harness that resolves a decision address, checks card mounting and ask rendering, and optionally exercises the take path to verify state flip and re-render.
intention_for_embedding: To let a lead validate a decision-card fork end-to-end in the live surface with per-leg verdicts, without risking real decisions unless explicitly testing a throwaway.

symbols.does: Async entry point that runs the full decision prove-on-one loop and returns a per-leg verdict.
symbols.description: proveDecision fetches the decision record, detects whether a real card is already mounted or creates a synthetic stub, checks for the host overlay and in-card ask, and optionally drives the take path and verifies the resulting state change and re-render.

symbols.does: Returns a promise that resolves after the given milliseconds.
symbols.description: A small delay helper used to give the host/DOM time to mount or re-render between legs.

symbols.does: Appends a leg result object to the legs array.
symbols.description: Records each leg's name, boolean/null outcome, and detail payload so the final verdict can summarize the run.

symbols.does: Computes overall pass and returns the final result object.
symbols.description: done filters out skipped legs (ok === null) and returns pass only when every checked leg is true, along with the detected mode and the full legs list.
